details.(a) Projection model + circle equation [: 15/15 — Correctly sets up the pinhole model with image plane z=-f, derives x=-fX/Z and y=-fY/Z via ray–plane intersection. For a circle on Z=Z0, substitutes X,Y in terms of x,y and rigorously obtains a circle equation in the image with correct center (x_c=-fX0/Z0, y_c=-fY0/Z0) and radius scaling r=fR/|Z0|. Logic is complete and equations are consistent.
details.(b) Vanishing point + special cases [15]: 14/15 — Derives vanishing point as t→∞ for lines P0+t(l,m,n), yielding (x_v,y_v)=(-f l/n, -f m/n) (i.e., -f*d_x/d_z, -f*d_y/d_z) with the n≠0 condition and notes the n=0 case (point at infinity). Verification includes at least two explicit plane cases (Y=0 gives y_v=0; X=0 gives x_v=0) plus additional checks. Minor issue: the statement of the task ties direction to a given plane but the vanishing-point formula itself does not depend on (A,B,C); the report handles this correctly but does not explicitly connect to the plane equation beyond the parallel-to-plane constraint.
details.(c) Vanishing line equation [10]: 8/10 — Provides a clear algebraic proof of collinearity: uses Al+Bm+Cn=0 and substitutes l,m from x_v,y_v to derive the vanishing line. However, the required equation is Ax_v+By_v+Cf=0, while the report derives Ax_v+By_v−Cf=0 under its sign convention and then states the +Cf version as an alternative; this creates ambiguity relative to the rubric’s fixed target form.

details.DoG/LoG [10]: 1/10 — The report only lists “multi-scale Gaussian pyramid construction” and “Difference of Gaussian (DoG) extrema detection” as goals, but provides no explanation of how DoG approximates LoG, no derivation (e.g., ∂G/∂σ ≈ σ∇²G), and no efficiency discussion about subtracting adjacent Gaussians vs. computing second derivatives.
details.Extrema+Contrast+Hessian [10]: 1/10 — It mentions DoG extrema detection and “low-contrast rejection and edge-response suppression” at a high level, but gives none of the required specifics: no 3x3x3 (26-neighbor) extrema test description, no contrast threshold formula |D(x)|>T, and no Hessian-based edge suppression criterion Tr(H)^2/Det(H) < (r+1)^2/r with Det(H)>0.
details.Orientation assignment [8]: 1/8 — It only states “dominant orientation assignment” as a stage, without describing the 36-bin histogram, Gaussian weighting, histogram smoothing, peak interpolation, or multi-peak handling.
details.128-D descriptor [7]: 1/7 — It only states “128-dimensional descriptor” as a stage, without detailing the 16x16→4x4×8=128 construction, rotation alignment method, trilinear interpolation, or normalization/truncation/renormalization.
